fact: November 12 is exactly 316 days into the year - that's almost like counting to 316 Mississippi's in a row!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when squirrels go absolutely bonkers burying nuts everywhere, sometimes forgetting where they put half of them.
The sun sets before 5 PM in most northern places around this date, making it feel like bedtime when you're still doing homework.
November 12 falls smack in the middle of Scorpio season, when the sky's most dramatic constellation is showing off overhead.
Tree leaves are basically having their grand finale right now - maple trees turn colors so bright they look like they're on fire.
In Australia and other southern places, this is when jacaranda trees explode with purple flowers that carpet the ground like nature's confetti.
The air temperature around this date creates perfect conditions for morning frost that makes spider webs look like they're covered in tiny diamonds.
Deer are in their mating season right now, which means male deer are basically showing off and acting tough like they're in a nature reality show.
November 12 sits exactly 39 days before winter solstice in the north, making it the perfect time for shadows to get super long and spooky.
Geese flying south create their famous V-formations around this time, and they're so loud you can hear them honking from miles away.
The moon around mid-November reflects off bare tree branches, creating shadows that look like skeleton hands reaching across the ground.
In cold places, ponds start forming their first thin ice sheets that make satisfying cracking sounds when you step near them.
